---
title: "March Madness"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(bestglm)
library(dplyr)

```

```{r}
#reading in data
bball_data <- read.csv("cbb.csv", header = TRUE)

head(bball_data)
str(bball_data)

bball_set <- bball_data |>
  mutate(win_pct = W / G) |>
  select(ADJOE, ADJDE, EFG_O, EFG_D, X2P_O, X2P_D, X3P_O, X3P_D, ADJ_T, TOR, TORD, ORB, DRB, win_pct)

head(bball_set)

```

### EDA For College Basketball Regression Analysis

##### 

```{r}

ggplot(bball_set, aes(x=win_pct))+
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05, color = "black", fill = "skyblue")+
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Win Percentage",
    subtitle = "NCAA D1 Mens College Basketball",
    x = "Single Season Win Percentage",
    y = "Count"
  )
cor_matrix <- cor(bball_set, use = "complete.obs")  
corrplot::corrplot(cor_matrix, type = "upper")                   

tstmodel <- lm(win_pct ~ EFG_O + EFG_D + X2P_D + X2P_O + X3P_O + X3P_D + ORB + DRB + TOR, data = bball_set)
smplemodel <- lm(win_pct ~ EFG_O + X3P_D + ORB + DRB +TOR + X2P_D, data = bball_set)


pot_data <- bball_set |>
  select(EFG_O, X3P_D, X2P_D, ORB, DRB, TOR, win_pct)

cor_matrix2 <- cor(pot_data, use = "complete.obs")
corrplot::corrplot(cor_matrix2, type = "upper")
summary(tstmodel)
summary(smplemodel)
```

### Model Selection

#### Variable Inflation Factor

```{r}
#VIF testing
library(car)
vif_value <- vif(smplemodel)
print(vif_value)
mean(vif_value)
```

Model Selection using LASSO, RIDGE, Best Subsets

```{r}

#AIC
Best_AIC <- bestglm(bball_set, IC = "AIC")
print(Best_AIC$BestModel)
summary(Best_AIC$BestModel)

#BIC
Best_BIC <- bestglm(bball_set, IC = "BIC")
print(Best_BIC$BestModel)
summary(Best_BIC$BestModel)
```

```{r}
#LASSO
library(glmnet)
library(Matrix)

x <- model.matrix(win_pct ~ EFG_O + EFG_D + X2P_D + X2P_O + X3P_O + X3P_D + ORB + DRB + TOR, data = bball_set)
y <- bball_set$win_pct

set.seed(123)
cv_lasso <- cv.glmnet(x, y, alpha = 1)
lambda1se <- cv_lasso$lambda.1se
lasso_model <- glmnet(x, y, alpha = 1, lambda = lambda1se)

coef(lasso_model)
```

LASSO = EFG_O + EFG_D + X2P_O + X3P_D + ORB + DRB + TOR

BIC = ADJDE + EFG_O + EFG_D + X2P_D + ADJ_T + TOR + TORD + ORB + DRB

AIC = ADJDE + EFG_O + EFG_D + X2P_D + ADJ_T + TOR + TORD + ORB + DRB

```         
```

### Cross Validation to determine Best Model Fit

```{r}
set.seed(123)
n_iterations <- 100

mse_results <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_iterations, ncol = 4)
colnames(mse_results) <- c("OLS", "BestSubset_AIC", "BestSubset_BIC", "LASSO")

for (i in 1:n_iterations) {
  sample_idx <- sample(nrow(bball_set), 0.8 * nrow(bball_set))
  train_data <- bball_set[sample_idx, ]
  test_data <- bball_set[-sample_idx, ]
  
  x_train <- model.matrix(win_pct ~ EFG_O + EFG_D + X2P_D + X2P_O + X3P_O + X3P_D + ORB + DRB + TOR, data=train_data)[,-1]
y_train <- train_data$win_pct
x_test <- model.matrix(win_pct ~ EFG_O + EFG_D + X2P_D + X2P_O + X3P_O + X3P_D + ORB + DRB + TOR, data=test_data)[,-1]
y_test <- test_data$win_pct
  
  
  ols_model <- lm(win_pct ~ EFG_O + EFG_D + X2P_D + X2P_O + X3P_O + X3P_D + ORB + DRB + TOR, data=train_data)
  ols_preds <- predict(ols_model, test_data)
  mse_results[i, "OLS"] <- mean((y_test - ols_preds)^2)
  
  
  train_subset <- train_data[, c("EFG_O", "EFG_D", "X2P_D", "X2P_O", "X3P_O", "X3P_D", "ORB", "DRB", "TOR", "win_pct")]
  best_aic <- bestglm(train_subset, IC="AIC")$BestModel
  aic_preds <- predict(best_aic, test_data)
  mse_results[i, "BestSubset_AIC"] <- mean((y_test - aic_preds)^2)
  
  
  best_bic <- bestglm(train_subset, IC="BIC")$BestModel
  bic_preds <- predict(best_bic, test_data)
  mse_results[i, "BestSubset_BIC"] <- mean((y_test - bic_preds)^2)
  
  
  cv_lasso <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 1)
  lasso_preds <- predict(cv_lasso, s=cv_lasso$lambda.1se, newx=x_test)
  mse_results[i, "LASSO"] <- mean((y_test - lasso_preds)^2)
}

avg_mse <- colMeans(mse_results)
print("Average Out-of-Sample MSE for each Model:")
print(avg_mse)
```

```{r}
lasso <- lm(win_pct ~ EFG_O + EFG_D + X2P_O + X3P_D + ORB + DRB + TOR, data = bball_set)
summary(lasso)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

march_ball <- march_ball %>%
  mutate(GamesWon = case_when(
    POSTSEASON == "R64" ~ 0,
    POSTSEASON == "R32" ~ 1,
    POSTSEASON == "S16" ~ 2,
    POSTSEASON == "E8"  ~ 3,
    POSTSEASON == "F4"  ~ 4,
    POSTSEASON == "2ND" ~ 5,
    POSTSEASON == "Champions" ~ 6,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_  # Handles unexpected values
  ))

upsets <- march_ball |>
  filter(SEED == 11 | SEED == 12 | SEED == 13 | SEED == 14 | SEED == 15 | SEED == 16) |>
  filter(GamesWon >= 1) 
  

ggplot(data = upsets, aes(x = CONF, y = GamesWon, fill = CONF)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +  # Aggregate GamesWon for each Conference
  labs(title = "Tournament Upsets: Games Won by Conference",
       x = "Conference",
       y = "Total Games Won") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))  # Rotates x-axis labels


ggplot() +
  # First dataset: All teams (march_ball)
  geom_point(data = march_ball, aes(x = ADJDE, y = WinPCT), color = "blue", alpha = 0.5) +

  # Second dataset: Only upsets (upsets)
  geom_point(data = upsets, aes(x = ADJDE, y = WinPCT), color = "red", alpha = 0.8, size = 3) +

 
  theme_minimal()


ggplot(data = march_ball, aes(x = ORB, y = DRB))+
  geom_point()
```

```{r}
best_model <- Best_AIC$BestModel
plot(best_model)

summary(best_model)

library(corrplot)
pairs(bball_set[,c("ADJDE", "EFG_O", "EFG_D", "X2P_D", "ADJ_T", "TOR", "TORD", "ORB", "DRB")], main = "Scatter Plot Matrix for AIC Best Model Fit")

pairs(bball_set[, c("EFG_O", "EFG_D", "X2P_O", "X3P_D", "ORB", "DRB", "TOR")], main = "Scatterplot Matrix: LASSO")

Multico <- vif(best_model)
print(Multico)
mean(Multico)
```

```{r}
new_model1 <- lm(win_pct ~ ADJDE + EFG_O + EFG_D + ADJ_T + TOR + TORD + ORB + DRB, data = bball_set)
summary(new_model1)

new_model2 <- lm(win_pct ~ ADJDE + EFG_O + X2P_D + ADJ_T + TOR + TORD + ORB + DRB, data = bball_set)
summary(new_model2)

mod1 <- vif(new_model1)
mod2 <- vif(new_model2)

print(mod1)
print(mod2)
mean(mod1)
```

```{r}
filtered <- bball_data |>
  filter(TEAM == "Houston") |>
  filter(YEAR == 2019)


```

```{r}
prediction <- predict(new_model1, newdata = filtered)
print(prediction)
```
